name: Zendo
highlight_text: Category: Best for productized & subscription services
description:
Source: Zendo

What Zendo Does

Zendo is an agency billing software that offers subscription packages with a white-label client portal. Zendo helps agencies sell services like products, whether one-off, subscription, or custom. Its branded client portal combines chat, approvals, invoices, and payments.

Key Features

  • Service catalog + subscription billing.
  • White-label client portal with chat.
  • Automated quotes and invoices.

Pricing

  • Free tier available
  • Pro: $37/mo
  • Max: $149/mo
  • Enterprise: starts at $999/mo

Pros

✅ Excellent client portal + subscriptions flow.
✅ Free tier to test; transparent pricing.

✅ Cleaner, faster sales-to-payment journey.

Cons

🚫 Some users want more design/customization.

🚫 Setup can feel complex.

‍
what_people_say:
What Customers Say

“Zendo is a powerful productized services manager with a complete back-office. Easy to launch and onboard clients” 

- Review collected by and hosted on G2.com.
summing_up_description:
Billing with Excel might work while you’re small. But once retainers stack up and expenses get more complex, mistakes creep in. A proper marketing agency billing software keeps your books clean, cash flow predictable, and clients paying on time. Remember, the best agency billing software ticks five boxes:

  • Matches your billing model.
  • Automates recurring or milestone invoices.
  • Plays nice with your other tools.
  • Handles payments in multiple currencies.
  • Scales without extra admin work.

If your current setup can’t check those off, you’re going to feel the pain as you grow.

‍

Get Started with Synup

If you’re running a mid-sized agency, you might benefit from all that Synup OS brings to the table. Here’s what you can do with this billing software:

  • Share Trackable Documents: Send branded invoices and proposals with built-in e-signatures. Clients sign off without extra tools.
  • Automated Payment Followups: Set reminders once and let the system nudge clients. Payments flow in through Stripe, PayPal, and more.
  • Simplify Subscriptions: Bill monthly, quarterly, or annually. Perfect for retainers. Clients can even enable auto-pay.
  • Stay Globally-compliant: Smart reminders and integrations keep you on schedule and clients satisfied.

This combination reduces tool fatigue, saves finance teams hours, and makes your agency look more professional in every client interaction. Check out the OS’s invoicing and payments functionality and book a demo for a setup/walkthrough. 

‍

Best 7 Agency Billing Software: FAQs

  1. Which billing software works best for freelancers or solo consultants?

Freelancers usually need something lightweight with invoicing, contracts, and time tracking in one place. Tools like Bonsai are popular because they cover the basics in a single dashboard without overwhelming features. The key is choosing a platform that keeps admin simple so you can focus on client work.

  1. How do agencies that sell fixed packages or subscriptions handle billing?

Agencies offering set services like “Monthly Social Media Management” or “Content Bundles” benefit from platforms that support recurring and productized billing. Tools such as Zendo make it easy to turn packages into ready-to-purchase offers. The real advantage is consistency. Clients know exactly what they’re paying for, and you reduce billing friction.

  1. What’s the most scalable option for agencies growing large? 

Synup OS is built with mid-sized and scaling agencies in mind. It handles recurring and split invoices, integrates with payment gateways globally, and adds a white-label portal for clients. The automation features mean you don’t need to add headcount every time your client list grows.

‍
